headlines.title: R. R. MAIL EMPLOYES GET NEW INSTRUCTIONS
headlines.subtitle: Notices to Eleventh District Men Urges Caution in Handling Packages.
headlines.fullText: Fresh cautions to employes of the Railway Mail Hervice on care in han- Sun barer post packages were Issued Wednesday in the general orders of the Eleventh division of the service from Fort Worth headquarters. The orders are: Although cautionary notices and in- structions concerning the care and handling of parcel post packages have been issued from time to time, many patrons of the postal service still com- plain that parcels are being received in bad condition, and Investigations show that in some Instances the parcels were not properly packed and in others that rough handling of the malls. Postmas. Fare. Clerks. Carriers, railway postal clerks, and arma that the hela te striet account for damage to the mails for which they are responal- ble. Clerks assigned to duty at the win- dows should be required to familiarize themselves with amended section 474 of the postal laws and regulations (printed in the April, 1514, supplement to the Postal Guide), providing for the proper parking of mail matter, and in- structedy to accept for mailing only such parcels an are prepared in con- Tutte Pulky packages which can trans- ported in mail bags must be hand packed. Packages which can not be safely handled in bags must be handled out- Heavy packages, such as casting. pieces of machinery, etc., must not be placed in bags with other mail matter which they are likely to damage on account of their weight. Fragile parcels must be kept sepa- rate, so far as practicable, from other mail, and bags containing fragile par- cels must have "Fragile" tags attached to them. Parcel post packages liable to dam- age must not be thrown from moving masters to traina which do not stop at their stationa. All parcels must be promptly han- ded and dispatched, and special care must be used to prevent delay to per- ishable articlea. Parcel post mail must not be thrown from cara,wagons or Trucks, but must ha lifted theretrom, and it must be lift. ed inte ears and wagons. Parcel post mall must not be thrown down chutes embankments ete. but gown res at from place to place. It must be piled that fragile parcela will not be under- neath other mail and must not be sat or stood upon. Officers and employes are expected to pay particular attention to the way mail is handled and promptly report violations of these Instructions. Em- ployes under the jurisdiction of the postmaster who fall to use due care in the handling of parce post mail Been made man ploves Mall through messengers an official and bulletin contractors no- and their employes, including employes railroad companies, must handle of Famesu cuer care Thereliction on the part of an employe not under the direct supervision of the postmaster reau should of be the reported to the proper bu- reau of the department."

headlines.title: MEXICAN IS HELD TO LIFE SENTENCE
headlines.subtitle: Court of Appeals Affirms Case Where Sixteen Others Are Accused of Murder.
headlines.fullText: By Amezcleted Press. AUSTIN, June 4-The court triminal appeals yesterday affirmed the case of Jesus Gonzales from La Salle county. Gonzales is one of the seven- teen Mexicans who were implicated In the murder of Deputy Sheriff Or- tis of Dimmit county, at which time the dead man was with a posse at- tempting to capture the Mexican band of alleged smugglers. The court gave Gonzales a life term. It also affirmed the case of Sam Truett, who was given ten emoon, viction of the murder of a man named Brigham in Hill county. Affirmance was also had in the case of George Hamilton, five years for rape, from Eastland county. The court also affirmed the case of W. A. Collmorgen from Angelina county. headlines.title: DANIEL BAKER "GRADS" GIVEN B. A. DEGREE
headlines.subtitle: Will H. Mayes and Others Honored by Board of Trustees of Brown.
headlines.fullText: BROWNWOOD. Texas, June 4. The twenty-fifth annual commencement ex- ercises of Daniel Baker college were held in the college chapel yesterday. The following graduates were given the degree of bachelor of arts: Ora Freeland. Emmett Young Com Ray Alonzo MeLeskey. Ben-Till Moore, Kaura Adra Om Brevard Steel, Toff stemmer sephus Cavitt Young: bachelor ature, Beatrice Mcmaster cates ,or cu esti- Allee Stewart, piano: Mr. I. C. Bagwell, The board of trustees awarded the foilewing honorar degrootste lace- Doctor Prof. Greer, temporary of president divinity, of Rev. W. B. Gray, Brownwood; Rev. B. E. Wallace, San Angelo, and C. R. Wright, Coralcana. The address to the graduating class was delivered by Sam Streetman of Houston. The year just closed at Daniel Baker Is the most successful in the history of the word forty-nine students enrolled, from .. tony chin; eight Texas counties and different staten.
